scopeBoundary: The repository engineering checker freezes the exact retained six-package classification, runtime dependency declarations, workspace and Node imports, manifest loading fields, relative containment, recognized production-source module forms, and explicitly guarded loader bypass syntax. It does not redefine dependency authority or grant product execution authority.

futureCheckClasses.description: Any future package, entry point, edge, source extension, or loading syntax requires an explicit Machine Task Contract and matching deterministic checker tests.
No inference package exists in the retained six-package surface. A future inference package requires explicit isolation checks before it can be classified.
The loading guard does not prove semantic absence of private Verdict, Evidence, Evidence Requirement, Policy, Rule, or Evidence Bundle definitions in delivery code.

partialEnforcementStatement: PRODUCT-HARDEN-001 closes the observable current six-package loading-form gaps enumerated by the bounded checker. The result remains a repository engineering drift guard, not general module resolution, transitive or generated-code analysis, target-repository inspection, inference-isolation proof, delivery-definition analysis, or complete architecture quality enforcement.
